label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what's true about lightning according to the passage. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the details. The passage starts by talking about how even though there wasn't much rain in April, parents should still be able to explain thunder and lightning to kids because many adults don't fully understand them. Then it mentions that lightning we see flashing down from a cloud is actually flashing up from the earth. So that's a point where the common perception is different from the reality. Next, it says most lightning flashes happen inside a cloud, and only a few are between clouds or between earth and cloud. Then there's a part about 2000 thunderstorms happening every minute, producing about 100 lightning strikes on earth every second. The options are A, B, C, D. Let's break them down. Option A: "Only a small number of lightning flashes occur on earth." The passage says most lightning is inside clouds, and only a few between clouds or earth/cloud. So the number that actually strike the earth would be a small portion of all lightning flashes. But the option says "occur on earth." Wait, but if most are inside clouds, then maybe the ones that strike the earth are a small number compared to total lightning flashes. But the passage says "only a few can be seen jumping between two clouds or between earth and a cloud." So the total number of lightning strikes on earth would be part of that "few." So if most lightning is inside clouds, then the number that hits the earth is a small part. But the option says "only a small number of lightning flashes occur on earth." Wait, does it mean lightning flashes that occur on earth (i.e., earth-to-cloud) versus in clouds? So if most lightning is in clouds, then yes, the ones on earth (earth-to-cloud or cloud-to-earth) are a small number. So A might be correct, but let me check the other options. Option B: "Lightning flashes usually jump from one cloud to another." The passage says "most lightning flashes take place inside a cloud, and only a few can be seen jumping between two clouds or between earth and a cloud." So if most are inside clouds, then the ones between clouds (cloud to cloud) are part of the "few." Therefore, B is incorrect because it says "usually," implying most, but the passage says most are inside a single cloud. Option C: "Lightning travels 5 times faster than thunder."
